title: Ruta besseri
taxonomy: Ruta besseri is classified within the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the citrus or rue family. It belongs to the genus Ruta, which includes several species of flowering plants native to the Mediterranean region. Ruta besseri is a species of perennial herb, closely related to other species such as Ruta graveolens, the common rue. The plant's classification is further supported by its botanical features, including its leaf shape and arrangement. Within the Rutaceae family, Ruta besseri is one of the lesser-known species.

intro: Ruta graveolens, also known as Ruta besseri, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, commonly referred to as Rue. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties, offering potential health benefits in treating wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Ruta besseri include flavonoids, alkaloids, and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
